# TypeScript Strict Reference

Use this reference when defining props, API data, component state, or utility types.
Strict typing prevents bugs, improves IDE support, and makes code self-documenting.

---

## Core Rules

1. Never use `any`. Use `unknown` if the type is truly unknown, then narrow.
2. Never use unsafe casts (`as Type`) unless you can justify why the type system can't know the value.
3. Enable `strict: true` in `tsconfig.json`. Never weaken it.
4. Handle `null` and `undefined` explicitly — don't ignore optional values.
5. Prefer inference when it produces clear types. Add explicit annotations when inference is unclear or when defining public APIs.

---

## Props Typing

### Basic Props

```tsx
// React
interface UserCardProps {
  user: User;
  onSelect: (userId: string) => void;
  variant?: "compact" | "full";
  className?: string;
}

function UserCard({ user, onSelect, variant = "full", className }: UserCardProps) {
  return (
    <article className={className}>{/* ... */}</article>
  );
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
interface UserCardProps {
  user: User;
  variant?: "compact" | "full";
}

const props = withDefaults(defineProps<UserCardProps>(), {
  variant: "full",
});

const emit = defineEmits<{
  select: [userId: string];
}>();
</script>
```

### Extending HTML Element Props

```tsx
// React
interface ButtonProps extends React.ComponentProps<"button"> {
  variant: "primary" | "secondary" | "ghost";
  size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg";
  isLoading?: boolean;
}

function Button({ variant, size = "md", isLoading, children, ...props }: ButtonProps) {
  return (
    <button {...props} disabled={props.disabled || isLoading}>
      {isLoading ? <Spinner /> : children}
    </button>
  );
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue — use defineProps + $attrs for passthrough -->
<script setup lang="ts">
interface ButtonProps {
  variant: "primary" | "secondary" | "ghost";
  size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg";
  isLoading?: boolean;
}

const props = withDefaults(defineProps<ButtonProps>(), {
  size: "md",
});
</script>

<template>
  <button v-bind="$attrs" :disabled="$attrs.disabled || isLoading">
    <Spinner v-if="isLoading" />
    <slot v-else />
  </button>
</template>
```

### Polymorphic Components

```tsx
type PolymorphicProps<E extends React.ElementType> = {
  as?: E;
  children: React.ReactNode;
} & Omit<React.ComponentProps<E>, "as" | "children">;

function Container<E extends React.ElementType = "div">({
  as,
  children,
  ...props
}: PolymorphicProps<E>) {
  const Component = as || "div";
  return <Component {...props}>{children}</Component>;
}

// Usage
<Container as="section" aria-label="Features">...</Container>
<Container as="main">...</Container>
```

### Discriminated Union Props

Use when a component behaves differently based on a variant.

```tsx
type AlertProps =
  | { variant: "info"; message: string }
  | { variant: "action"; message: string; onAction: () => void; actionLabel: string }
  | { variant: "dismiss"; message: string; onDismiss: () => void };

function Alert(props: AlertProps) {
  switch (props.variant) {
    case "info":
      return <div role="status">{props.message}</div>;
    case "action":
      return (
        <div role="alert">
          {props.message}
          <button onClick={props.onAction}>{props.actionLabel}</button>
        </div>
      );
    case "dismiss":
      return (
        <div role="alert">
          {props.message}
          <button onClick={props.onDismiss} aria-label="Dismiss">×</button>
        </div>
      );
  }
}
```

---

## State Modeling

### Discriminated Union for Async State

Never model async state as independent booleans.

```tsx
// Wrong — impossible states are possible
interface UserState {
  isLoading: boolean;
  error: string | null;
  data: User | null;
}
// isLoading: true AND error: "..." AND data: {...} — what does this mean?

// Correct — each state is explicit
type UserState =
  | { status: "idle" }
  | { status: "loading" }
  | { status: "error"; error: string }
  | { status: "success"; data: User };

function UserProfile({ state }: { state: UserState }) {
  switch (state.status) {
    case "idle":
      return null;
    case "loading":
      return <Skeleton />;
    case "error":
      return <ErrorMessage message={state.error} />;
    case "success":
      return <ProfileCard user={state.data} />;
  }
}
```

### Form State

```tsx
interface FormState<T> {
  values: T;
  errors: Partial<Record<keyof T, string>>;
  touched: Partial<Record<keyof T, boolean>>;
  status: "idle" | "submitting" | "submitted" | "error";
}

interface ContactForm {
  name: string;
  email: string;
  message: string;
}

const initialState: FormState<ContactForm> = {
  values: { name: "", email: "", message: "" },
  errors: {},
  touched: {},
  status: "idle",
};
```

---

## API Response Typing

### Defining API Types

```tsx
// Define the shape of your API responses
interface ApiResponse<T> {
  data: T;
  meta: {
    page: number;
    totalPages: number;
    totalCount: number;
  };
}

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  role: "admin" | "member" | "viewer";
  createdAt: string;
}

// Typed fetch function
async function fetchUsers(page: number): Promise<ApiResponse<User[]>> {
  const response = await fetch(`/api/users?page=${page}`);
  if (!response.ok) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to fetch users: ${response.status}`);
  }
  return response.json() as Promise<ApiResponse<User[]>>;
}
```

### Validating External Data

Never trust external data at runtime. Validate at the boundary.

```tsx
import { z } from "zod";

const UserSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string(),
  name: z.string(),
  email: z.string().email(),
  role: z.enum(["admin", "member", "viewer"]),
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
});

type User = z.infer<typeof UserSchema>;

const UsersResponseSchema = z.object({
  data: z.array(UserSchema),
  meta: z.object({
    page: z.number(),
    totalPages: z.number(),
    totalCount: z.number(),
  }),
});

async function fetchUsers(page: number) {
  const response = await fetch(`/api/users?page=${page}`);
  if (!response.ok) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to fetch users: ${response.status}`);
  }
  const json = await response.json();
  return UsersResponseSchema.parse(json);
}
```

---

## Branded Types

Prevent accidental misuse of primitive types that represent distinct domain concepts.

```tsx
// Define brands
type UserId = string & { readonly __brand: "UserId" };
type OrderId = string & { readonly __brand: "OrderId" };

// Constructor functions
function UserId(id: string): UserId {
  return id as UserId;
}

function OrderId(id: string): OrderId {
  return id as OrderId;
}

// Now this is a type error:
function getOrder(orderId: OrderId) { /* ... */ }

const userId = UserId("user_123");
const orderId = OrderId("order_456");

getOrder(userId); // Type error — UserId is not assignable to OrderId
getOrder(orderId); // Works
```

Use branded types for:
- Entity IDs (`UserId`, `OrderId`, `ProductId`)
- Currency values (`Cents`, `Dollars` — prevent math between different units)
- Validated strings (`Email`, `Url` — after validation)

---

## `satisfies` Operator

Use `satisfies` to validate a value matches a type without widening inference.

```tsx
// Without satisfies — type is widened to Record<string, ...>
const routes: Record<string, { path: string; title: string }> = {
  home: { path: "/", title: "Home" },
  about: { path: "/about", title: "About" },
};
routes.typo; // No error — any string key is valid

// With satisfies — validates AND preserves exact keys
const routes = {
  home: { path: "/", title: "Home" },
  about: { path: "/about", title: "About" },
} satisfies Record<string, { path: string; title: string }>;

routes.typo; // Type error — "typo" does not exist
routes.home.path; // Type is exactly "/"
```

Use `satisfies` for:
- Config objects where you want validation + autocomplete
- Theme definitions
- Route maps
- Feature flags

---

## `as const` Assertions

Use `as const` to infer the narrowest possible type.

```tsx
// Without as const
const STATUS = { ACTIVE: "active", INACTIVE: "inactive" };
// Type: { ACTIVE: string, INACTIVE: string }

// With as const
const STATUS = { ACTIVE: "active", INACTIVE: "inactive" } as const;
// Type: { readonly ACTIVE: "active", readonly INACTIVE: "inactive" }

// Extract union type from const object
type Status = (typeof STATUS)[keyof typeof STATUS]; // "active" | "inactive"

// Array to union
const ROLES = ["admin", "member", "viewer"] as const;
type Role = (typeof ROLES)[number]; // "admin" | "member" | "viewer"
```

---

## Template Literal Types

Use for route parameters, CSS values, event names, and string-based APIs.

```tsx
// Route parameters
type Route = `/users/${string}` | `/users/${string}/posts/${string}`;

function navigate(route: Route) { /* ... */ }
navigate("/users/123");            // Works
navigate("/users/123/posts/456");  // Works
navigate("/products/123");         // Type error

// CSS values
type CSSLength = `${number}${"px" | "rem" | "em" | "%"}`;
function setWidth(width: CSSLength) { /* ... */ }
setWidth("16px");  // Works
setWidth("1.5rem"); // Works
setWidth("16");    // Type error

// Event names
type EventName = `on${Capitalize<string>}`;
```

---

## Result Pattern for Error Handling

Model operations that can fail without relying on try/catch everywhere.

```tsx
type Result<T, E = string> =
  | { ok: true; data: T }
  | { ok: false; error: E };

function ok<T>(data: T): Result<T, never> {
  return { ok: true, data };
}

function err<E>(error: E): Result<never, E> {
  return { ok: false, error };
}

// Usage
async function parseCSV(file: File): Promise<Result<Row[], "INVALID_FORMAT" | "TOO_LARGE">> {
  if (file.size > MAX_SIZE) return err("TOO_LARGE");

  try {
    const rows = await readAndParse(file);
    return ok(rows);
  } catch {
    return err("INVALID_FORMAT");
  }
}

// Consumer
const result = await parseCSV(file);
if (!result.ok) {
  switch (result.error) {
    case "TOO_LARGE":
      showError("File exceeds the 10MB limit.");
      break;
    case "INVALID_FORMAT":
      showError("Could not parse the file. Please check the format.");
      break;
  }
  return;
}
// result.data is Row[] here
renderTable(result.data);
```

---

## Narrowing and Type Guards

### typeof and instanceof

```tsx
function formatValue(value: string | number): string {
  if (typeof value === "number") {
    return value.toFixed(2);
  }
  return value;
}
```

### Custom Type Guards

```tsx
interface SuccessResult {
  status: "success";
  data: User;
}

interface ErrorResult {
  status: "error";
  error: string;
}

type Result = SuccessResult | ErrorResult;

function isSuccess(result: Result): result is SuccessResult {
  return result.status === "success";
}
```

### Exhaustive Checks

```tsx
type Status = "active" | "inactive" | "pending";

function getStatusColor(status: Status): string {
  switch (status) {
    case "active":
      return "green";
    case "inactive":
      return "gray";
    case "pending":
      return "yellow";
    default: {
      const _exhaustive: never = status;
      return _exhaustive;
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Utility Types

### Common Patterns

```tsx
// Make specific fields optional
type CreateUserInput = Omit<User, "id" | "createdAt">;

// Make specific fields required
type UserWithAvatar = User & { avatarUrl: string };

// Partial update
type UpdateUserInput = Partial<Pick<User, "name" | "email">>;

// Record for maps
type UserPermissions = Record<string, boolean>;

// Extract from union
type ActiveUser = Extract<UserState, { status: "success" }>;
```

### Mapped Types

```tsx
// Make all properties readonly
type Immutable<T> = {
  readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K];
};

// Make all properties nullable
type Nullable<T> = {
  [K in keyof T]: T[K] | null;
};
```

---

## Event Handler Typing

```tsx
// React
function handleSubmit(event: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) {
  event.preventDefault();
  const formData = new FormData(event.currentTarget);
}

function handleChange(event: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) {
  setValue(event.target.value);
}

function handleKeyDown(event: React.KeyboardEvent<HTMLInputElement>) {
  if (event.key === "Enter") { submit(); }
}

// Generic callback props
interface SearchProps {
  onSearch: (query: string) => void;
  onSelect: (item: SearchResult) => void;
  onError?: (error: Error) => void;
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue — native DOM events, no React wrappers needed -->
<script setup lang="ts">
function handleSubmit(event: Event) {
  event.preventDefault();
  const form = event.target as HTMLFormElement;
  const formData = new FormData(form);
}

function handleInput(event: Event) {
  const target = event.target as HTMLInputElement;
  query.value = target.value;
}

// Emits as typed callbacks
const emit = defineEmits<{
  search: [query: string];
  select: [item: SearchResult];
  error: [error: Error];
}>();
</script>

<template>
  <form @submit="handleSubmit">
    <input @input="handleInput" @keydown.enter="emit('search', query)" />
  </form>
</template>
```

---

## Generic Components

```tsx
interface ListProps<T> {
  items: T[];
  renderItem: (item: T) => React.ReactNode;
  keyExtractor: (item: T) => string;
  emptyMessage?: string;
}

function List<T>({ items, renderItem, keyExtractor, emptyMessage }: ListProps<T>) {
  if (items.length === 0 && emptyMessage) {
    return <p>{emptyMessage}</p>;
  }

  return (
    <ul role="list">
      {items.map((item) => (
        <li key={keyExtractor(item)}>{renderItem(item)}</li>
      ))}
    </ul>
  );
}

// Usage — T is inferred
<List
  items={users}
  keyExtractor={(user) => user.id}
  renderItem={(user) => <UserCard user={user} />}
  emptyMessage="No users found"
/>
```

---

## What Not to Do

```tsx
// Don't use any
function processData(data: any) { ... }
// Use: function processData(data: unknown) { ... }

// Don't use non-null assertion unless you're certain
const name = user!.name;
// Use: const name = user?.name ?? "Unknown";

// Don't cast to silence errors
const items = data as Item[];
// Use: validate data at the boundary with zod or similar

// Don't use enum (prefer union types)
enum Status { Active, Inactive }
// Use: type Status = "active" | "inactive";

// Don't use namespace
namespace Utils { ... }
// Use: regular modules with named exports

// Don't use Function type
const callback: Function = () => {};
// Use: const callback: () => void = () => {};
```
